nodes.label: claude hooks integration
nodes.description: brAIn integrates with the Claude Code hook system to nudge users into keeping the graph in sync with the code. Three hooks: SessionStart (brain_session_start.sh) timestamps the session and clears tmp state; PostToolUse (brain_hook.sh) logs every Edit/Write target and injects a per-tool reminder for Claude; Stop (brain_stop_check.py) blocks once if files were modified without any graph update during the session.

nodes.label: brain session start hook
nodes.description: brain_session_start.sh — SessionStart hook. Writes the current UTC ISO timestamp to /tmp/brain_session_start.txt and removes any leftover /tmp/brain_session_modified.txt and /tmp/brain_stop_blocked.txt from a previous session. Provides the baseline timestamp that the Stop hook compares against Node.updated_at.

nodes.label: brain posttooluse hook
nodes.description: brain_hook.sh — PostToolUse hook fired after every Edit/Write. Pipes stdin through jq to extract tool_input.file_path / tool_input.new_file_path, appends the path to /tmp/brain_session_modified.txt, then emits a hookSpecificOutput JSON object that injects 'this file was modified; if it contains a structural decision update the graph with brain_find + brain_ingest' as additionalContext for Claude. The reminder is suppressOutput-flagged so it does not clutter the terminal.

nodes.label: brain stop check hook
nodes.description: brain_stop_check.py — Stop hook. If files were modified during the session but no Node.updated_at is >= session_start, returns {continue: false, stopReason: 'review your changes for structural decisions before terminating'} so Claude is forced to reconsider. Marks the session as already-blocked via /tmp/brain_stop_blocked.txt; the second Stop invocation always allows so the user can override after Claude's review. Listed files capped at 20 to keep the message readable.

nodes.label: session start timestamp file
nodes.description: /tmp/brain_session_start.txt — single line: UTC ISO timestamp written by the SessionStart hook. Read by the Stop hook to compare against Node.updated_at via a Cypher query. Falls back to (now - 1h) if the file is missing, so a session started before the hook was installed still has a sensible baseline.

nodes.label: modified files log file
nodes.description: /tmp/brain_session_modified.txt — append-only list of every file path touched by Edit/Write during the session. Written by the PostToolUse hook, read by the Stop hook. Cleaned up by SessionStart (next session) and by the Stop hook on cleanup() path. The presence-of-content is what triggers the Stop block.

nodes.label: stop blocked marker file
nodes.description: /tmp/brain_stop_blocked.txt — sentinel file. Written when the Stop hook blocks a session for the first time. Checked at the top of the next Stop invocation: if present, allow unconditionally (and clean up). Implements the 'block once, then trust' policy — Claude only sees the prompt once, the user can finish the session after reviewing.

nodes.label: block once then trust policy
nodes.description: Design choice for the Stop hook: it blocks at MOST ONCE per session. Rationale: a hard block would frustrate users on small refactors that genuinely don't need graph updates. A single block-and-prompt is enough to bring graph-update intent into Claude's working context without turning the hook into an obstacle.

nodes.label: graph update detection query
nodes.description: Stop hook query: MATCH (n:Node) WHERE n.updated_at >= $start RETURN count(n). If any node was updated since session_start, the hook treats the session as graph-synced and allows termination. NOT a strict 'changes match' check — any graph activity satisfies the gate; the assumption is that if Claude touched the graph at all during the session, it has already engaged with the structural-decision question.

nodes.label: hook failure is silent pass
nodes.description: Stop hook wraps the Kuzu query in try/except and on any exception calls cleanup() and exits 0. Rationale: a broken DB or stale schema must NOT prevent the user from ending a session. The hook is a nudge, not a gate — if it cannot operate, it stays out of the way.

nodes.label: skill command brain status
nodes.description: .claude/commands/brain-status.md — slash command 'brain-status' available in Claude Code. Runs `brain stats` and `brain audit`, summarizes counts and warnings. Idiomatic 'where is the graph right now' check at the start of a session.

nodes.label: skill command brain query
nodes.description: .claude/commands/brain-query.md — slash command 'brain-query' that takes a question, maps it to the right CLI command (find / causes / effects / paths / show / query), and asks Claude to cite the evidence field and flag chains with confidence below 0.6.

nodes.label: skill command ingest
nodes.description: .claude/commands/ingest.md — slash command 'ingest' that walks Claude through the 6-step SKILL.md ingestion workflow for a given document: read in full, extract 10-50 nodes with comprehensive descriptions, extract relations with rich evidence, anti-dup via `brain find`, save and ingest, verify with stats+audit.
